faq.question: Why do sailors use nautical miles?
faq.answer: A nautical mile equals one minute of latitude, making it directly tied to Earth's geometry. This makes navigation calculations on charts straightforward — 1 degree of latitude always equals 60 nautical miles.

faq.question: What is the difference between a knot and mph?
faq.answer: A knot is one nautical mile per hour (1.15 mph). So 10 knots = 11.5 mph = 18.5 km/h. The term comes from counting knots on a line dragged behind sailing ships to measure speed.

faq.question: How long is a nautical mile?
faq.answer: A nautical mile is 6,076 feet (1,852 meters), compared to a statute mile at 5,280 feet (1,609 meters). A nautical mile is about 15% longer than a statute mile.

faq.question: Is the Nautical Mile Converter free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard boating and marine formulas trusted by boaters and sailors. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Nautical Mile Converter?
sections.body: The Nautical Mile Converter is a free online tool designed for boaters and sailors who need quick, accurate calculations in the boating and marine space. By entering your distance value, from unit, you get instant results including nautical miles, statute miles, kilometers. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting nautical miles right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In boating and marine, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by boaters and sailors worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to navigate safely with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your distance value and need to find the right nautical miles. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
